description:
You are an infrastructure engineer automating a very small provisioning workflow.  
  Your goal is to use the SQLite CLI to create a database that tracks three servers and exports a plain-text list of those servers.

  Required final state of the system
  ----------------------------------
  1. Directory:
       /home/user/provisioning
       • It must exist by the end of the task.  
         (Create it if it is missing.)

  2. SQLite database:
       /home/user/provisioning/infra.db
       • Must contain exactly one table named servers defined with:
           id        INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
           hostname  TEXT           NOT NULL,
           ip        TEXT           NOT NULL
       • Exactly three rows must be present (ids must be 1, 2, 3 in that order):
           (1,'web-01'  ,'10.0.0.10')
           (2,'db-01'   ,'10.0.0.20')
           (3,'cache-01','10.0.0.30')

  3. Text export file:
       /home/user/provisioning/server_list.txt
       • Must be produced with the SQLite `.mode list` default pipe (|) separator  
         and without any header line.  
         • Exact required contents (including newline layout):

           web-01|10.0.0.10
           db-01|10.0.0.20
           cache-01|10.0.0.30

         The file must end with a trailing newline after the last line.

  4. Verification hint:
       The automated checker will open infra.db and read the servers
       table, then compare it against the expected three rows.
       It will also read server_list.txt and compare its byte-for-byte
       contents with the text block above.

  Your tools
  ----------
  • Bash shell with coreutils.  
  • The `sqlite3` command-line tool is pre-installed.

  Task summary
  ------------
  a) Make sure /home/user/provisioning exists.  
  b) Using SQLite CLI, create /home/user/provisioning/infra.db containing the servers table and the three rows shown.  
  c) Still inside SQLite, export the list of hostname and ip (in that order, ordered by id ascending) to /home/user/provisioning/server_list.txt using the exact pipe-separated format described above.  
  d) Confirm, via the CLI, that the file and the database exist in the correct location.

  Constraints
  -----------
  • You may use at most 2-3 SQLite commands inside the interactive SQLite session (for instance “CREATE TABLE”, three “INSERT” statements, and a single “.output …; SELECT …; .exit”).  
  • Do not add any additional tables, files, or extraneous rows.  
  • Ensure all commands run without requiring root privileges.  

  When everything is correct, the testing framework will read the database and the text file to grade your work.
truth:
<!-- Directory that must exist -->
  /home/user/provisioning

  <!-- SQLite DB that must exist and its expected schema -->
  File: /home/user/provisioning/infra.db
    Table: servers
      Columns:
        id        INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
        hostname  TEXT
        ip        TEXT
      Rows (ordered by id):
        1 | web-01  | 10.0.0.10
        2 | db-01   | 10.0.0.20
        3 | cache-01| 10.0.0.30

  <!-- Plain-text export file that must exist -->
  File: /home/user/provisioning/server_list.txt
    Exact contents (with trailing newline):
web-01|10.0.0.10
db-01|10.0.0.20
cache-01|10.0.0.30
name: task_000000_8a91615f
